thresholdSource: No lab panic value established for vitamin B12 (no cited acute critical-value source found). plausibleHigh 6500: above a documented serum cobalamin level of 6173 pg/mL caused by macro-B12 (cobalamin bound to anti-intrinsic-factor antibody complexes), where the patient's true underlying B12 status was normal ('Diagnostic challenge of high vitamin B12 levels: a case report and review of the literature,' Journal of Medical Case Reports, DOI 10.1186/s13256-026-05898-z). Accuracy bound only. No criticalLow/plausibleLow — no acute-low citable source found. Draft — not clinician-approved.
